source_metadata.title_raw: Nancy Diamond, Esq. - A-18-186 - November 6, 2018 - Arcata

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 6, 2018

Nancy Diamond, Esq.
General Counsel
Redwood Coast Energy Authority
822 G Street, Suite 3
Arcata, CA 95521

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-18-186

Dear Ms. Diamond:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act") and Government Code Section 1090 on behalf of the Chair of the Redwood Coast Energy Authority, Sheri Woo.¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code.

Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Humboldt County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Does Section 1090 prohibit the Redwood Coast Energy Authority Board of Directors ("RCEA") from entering into a purchase agreement of wholesale electricity with Humboldt Wind, LLC due to Chair Sheri Woo's financial interest in her employer, SHN Engineers & Geologists, Inc., whose services have been retained to perform geologic studies for Humboldt Wind?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-186
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

No. Chair Woo has only a remote interest in the resulting contract under Section
1091(b)(17), due to the fact that she is an owner of SHN Engineers & Geologists and serving as an
appointed member of RCEA's unelected Board, so long as she discloses her financial interest in the
agreement to RCEA, the interest is noted in the official records, and she recuses herself from
providing any advice to RCEA regarding the agreement.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

Sheri Woo was elected to the Humboldt Bay Municipal Water District Board of Directors
("HBMWD"). HBMWD moved to appoint her as representative to the Redwood Coast Energy
Authority Board of Directors ("RCEA"). RCEA is comprised of nine local government agencies in
Humboldt County – seven municipalities, the County of Humboldt, and a regional water district. A
representative from each member agency serves on the RCEA governing Board of Directors. Chair
Woo has served on the RCEA Board since 2012 and became its Chair in December of 2016.

Pursuant to one of RCEA's purposes, RCEA purchases wholesale electricity on behalf of
the electric customers within its jurisdiction, serving a function that replaces an investor-owned
utility company. At issue is a potential electric power purchase agreement between RCEA, which
would be approved by its governing Board of Directors, and an electric energy producer, Humboldt
Wind, LLC.

Humboldt Wind proposes to build a wind energy farm in Humholdt County. Humboldt
Wind is a subsidiary of Terra-Gen, LLC. Terra-Gen has renewable energy projects throughout the
western U.S. Terra-Gen retained Chair Woo's employer, SHN Engineers & Geologists, Inc.
("SHN"), to perform geologic studies required for development of Humboldt Wind's wind energy
project.

Chair Woo has been employed by SHN since April 2014 as its marketing coordinator where
she assists in sales proposals, branding, and business development. SHN is a regional firm of fewer
than 100 employees that provides general engineering, geologic, surveying, and environmental
consulting services throughout Northern California and Southern Oregon.

SHN expects to complete its current scope of services with Terra-Gen by December 31,
2018, however, additional work could be added to the current scope. RCEA may begin negotiations
with Humboldt Wind over the terms of a power purchase agreement before the SHN-Terra-Gen
contract is completed. The contract between SHN and Terra-Gen is expected to bring SHN revenue
equal to approximately 0.32% of its gross earnings.

Although SHN is currently performing geologic studies required for development of
Humboldt Wind LLC's wind energy project, it would not be explicitly referenced by a purchase
agreement of wholesale electricity between RCEA and Humboldt Wind LLC. However, it is
possible that SHN's technical work could be used by Humboldt Wind in response to a request for
proposals solicited in advance of contract negotiations and award.

File No. A-18-186
Page No. 3

Chair Woo's marketing coordinator position is organized in SHN's Corporate Division,
together with the CEO, CFO, bookkeepers, billing staff, and information technology staff. Her work
duties do not include work in any capacity on the Terra-Gen contract, nor supervision of the SHN
employees who conduct work on this contract. Her work performance is not measured, and her
salary is not established, by the number of contracts or clients held by SHN, or by their economic
value. She receives more than $500 per year in income from SHN.

Chair Woo became an SHN shareholder in March 2017. Her shareholder interest is 1,000
shares, which has a value greater than $2,000 and is less than 1% of the total SHN outstanding
shares. The initial share purchase will be matched by SHN through additional stock contributions of
500 shares in the first and second years. After that, she may receive additional shares as part of an
annual bonus, but there is no guaranteed bonus and there is no other guaranteed annual return on
shares.

ANALYSIS

Section 1090:

Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities,
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. For Section 1090 to apply, the public
official in question must have a financial interest in the contract in question. Although the statute does
not specifically define "financial interest," an examination of case law and the statutory exceptions to
the basic prohibition indicate that the term is to be liberally interpreted. (See People v. Deysher (1934) 2
Cal.2d 141, 146.) Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the
official has a financial interest" (People v. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333), and officials are
deemed to have a financial interest in a contract if they might profit from it in any way. (Ibid.)
When the Section 1090 prohibition is applicable, the prohibition acts as an absolute bar to the
officer's governing body or board entering into the prohibited contract. (69 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 102
(1986).)

Employees have a financial interest in a contract that involves their employer, even where
the contract would not result in a change in income or directly involve the employee because an
employee has an overall interest in the financial success of the firm and continued employment. (84
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 161-62 (2001).) In the Khuu Advice Letter, a councilmember worked for a
firm that provided various consulting services to clients of the firm, some of whom had contracts
with the city that would likely need to be renewed during the councilmember's term. Section 1090
prohibits the city council from renewing these contracts where the councilmember will be
influenced by a desire to "maintain favorable ongoing relationships" with not only the firm that
employs him or her but also the clients of the firm seeking to renew a contract with the city. (Khuu
Advice Letter, No. I-14-107.)

Here, Chair Woo's income from SHN would not be directly derived from an
RCEA/Humboldt Wind purchase agreement contract, so RCEA's decisions relating to the purchase
agreement do not directly involve her employment at SHN or her income from SHN. However,
Chair Woo has a financial interest in SHN evidenced by her employment with the company.

File No. A-18-186
Page No. 4

Additionally, she is a shareholder in the company. The implications to her financial interest involved in the purchase agreement are sufficient to trigger Section 1090's prohibition.²

As a general rule, when Section 1090 is applicable to one member of a governing body of a public entity, the prohibition cannot be avoided by having the interested board member abstain; the entire governing body is precluded from entering into the contract. The Legislature, however, has created various statutory exceptions to Section 1090's prohibition where the interest involved is deemed a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091 or a "noninterest," as defined in Section 1091.5. If a remote interest is present, the contract may be made by the agency provided: (1) the officer discloses his or her financial interest in the contract to the public body; (2) the interest is noted in the body's official records, and; (3) the officer abstains from participating in the making of the contract. (See 84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen 158, 159 (2001).)

Section 1091(b)(17) states that a remote interest exception is warranted for "an owner or a partner of a firm serving as an appointed member of an unelected board or commission of the contracting agency if the owner or partner recuses himself or herself from providing any advice to the contracting agency regarding the contract between the firm and the contracting agency and from all participation in reviewing a project that results from that contract."

This remote interest was added in 2015 by Senate Bill 704 to address the issue where the broad construction of Section 1090 acts to limit the ability of professionals with relevant expertise to serve on boards and commissions and for such boards to perform their intended function. (Assembly Committee on Elections and Redistricting analysis of Senate Bill 704 as amended July 8, 2015.)

As an SHN stockholder serving as an appointed member of RCEA's unelected Board, Chair Woo has a remote interest in the purchase agreement. Thus, if she discloses her financial interest in the agreement to RCEA, the interest is noted in the official records, and she recuses herself from providing any advice to RCEA regarding the agreement, RCEA is permitted to contract with Humboldt Wind.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Ryan P. O'Connor
Counsel, Legal Division

RPOC:jgl

___________________________

² We also note that the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act would also require Chair Woo to recuse herself from any participation in the purchase agreement process because it will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on her financial interests. (Section 87100; Regulation 18700.)

sections.question:
Does Section 1090 prohibit the Redwood Coast Energy Authority Board of Directors 
("RCEA") from entering into a purchase agreement of wholesale electricity with Humboldt Wind, 
LLC due to Chair Sheri Woo's financial interest in her employer, SHN Engineers & Geologists, 
Inc., whose services have· been retained to perform geologic studies for Humboldt Wind?
sections.conclusion:
No. Chair Woo has only a remote interest in the resulting contract under Section 
1091 (b )( 17), due to the fact that she is an owner of SHN Engineers & Geologists and serving as an 
appointed member of RCEA's unelected Board, so long as she discloses her financial interest in the 
agreement to RCEA, the interest is noted in the official records, and she recuses herself from 
providing any advice to RCEA regarding the agreement.

sections.analysis:
Section 1090: 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. For Section 1090 to apply,, the public 
official in question must have a financial interest in the contract in question. Although the statute does 
not specifically define "financial interest," an examination of case law and the statutory exceptions to 
the basic prohibition indicate that the term is to be liberally interpreted. (See People v. Deysher (1934) 2

official has a financial interest" (People v. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333), and officials are 
deemed to have a financial interest in a contract if they might profit from it in any way. (Ibid.) 
When the Section 1090 prohibition is applicable, the prohibition acts as an absolute bar to the 
officer's governing body or board entering into the prohibited contract. (69 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 102 
(1986).) 
Employees have a financial interest in a contract that involves their employer, even where 
the contract would not result in a change in income or directly involve the employee because an 
employee has an overall interest in the financial success of the firm and continued employment. (84 
Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 161-162 (2001).) In the Khuu Advice Letter, a councilmember worked for a 
firm that provided various consulting services to clients of the firm, some of whom had contracts 
with the city that would likely need to be renewed during the councilmember's term. Section 1090 
prohibits the city council from renewing these contracts where the councilmember will be 
influenced by a desire to "maintain favorable ongoing relationships" with not only the firm that

Here, Chair Woo's income from SHN would not be directly derived from an

agreement do not directly involve her employment at SHN or her income from SHN. However,

Additionally, she is a shareholder in the company. The implications to her financial interest 
involved in the purchase agreement are sufficient to trigger Section 1090's prohibition.2 
As a general rule, when Section 1090 is applicable to one member of a governing body of a 
public entity, the prohibition cannot be avoided by having the interested board member abstain; the

noted in the body's official records, and; (3) the officer abstains from participating in the making of 
the contract. (See 84 Ops.C,al.Atty.Gen 158, 159 (2001).) 
Section 1091(b)(l 7) states that a remote interest exception is warranted for "an owner or a 
partner of a firm serving as an appointed member of an unelected board or commission of the 
contracting agency if the owner or partner recuses himself or herself from providing any advice to 
the contracting agency regarding the contract between the firm and the contracting agency and from 
all participation in reviewing a project that results from that contract." 
This remote interest was added in 2015 by Senate Bill 704 to address the issue where the 
broad construction of Section 1090 acts to limit the ability of professionals with relevant expertise 
to serve on boards and commissions and for such boards to perform their intended function. 
(Assembly Committee on Elections and Redistricting analysis of Senate Bill 704 as amended July 
8, 2015.) 
As an SHN stockholder serving as an appointed member of RCEA's unelected Board, Chair 
Woo has a remote interest in the purchase agreement. Thus, if she discloses her financial interest in 
the agreement to RCEA, the interest is noted in the official records, and she recuses herself from 
providing any advice to RCEA regarding the agreement, RCEA is permitted to contract with 
Humboldt Wind.
